title: salix rotundifolia subsp. dodgeana
intro: Salix rotundifolia subsp. dodgeana, also known as Roundleaf Willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which have been shown to be effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Salix rotundifolia subsp. dodgeana have been used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, making it beneficial for individuals suffering from arthritis, headaches, and other conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, and has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal and spiritual properties.
